rationale: All four independent lenses returned pass with no error-severity findings and no majority fail. Implementation meets the be-dev-test brief (CSV→SQLite import, paginated input-validated API, async Tailwind-Plus list view); 43 tests assert requirements and pass; setup runs from the README verbatim. Only info/warning polish items remain.

findings.message: Comprehension: all in-scope acceptance criteria met — idempotent CSV import (1000 rows, empties→NULL, unicode preserved), paginated GET /api/customers with full meta, 400 JSON on bad input, 200 empty page past end, async fetch-and-render list.

Tests: 43 tests pass and assert requirements (quoted-comma intact, empty→NULL, unicode, re-import determinism, page1!=page2, short/empty pages, 400 on garbage, pageSize clamp, XSS escaping incl. javascript: rejection).
Pagination math correct across edges; getCustomers reuses paginate() for offset; LIMIT/OFFSET parameter-bound; ordered by integer PK.
Cosmetic: status line computes from>to on a page past the end, but it is not user-reachable (Next disables on hasNext) and the body shows an empty-state message. Non-blocking.
All SQL parameterised (query + import); no string interpolation of input into SQL.
XSS: render.js escapes every value and only links http(s) URLs (javascript: shown as text); unit-tested.
Input validation rejects non-positive-integers (400) and clamps pageSize to 100, preventing unbounded scans.
a11y (semantic table, scope, aria-live/aria-busy, role=alert, real buttons, noscript) and SEO (title/desc/lang + acknowledged client-render trade-off) addressed appropriately.
DRY: offset math, the CSV column list, escaping, and per-cell Tailwind classes are each defined once. No duplicated logic.
Right-sized: createApp(db) factory and STATIC_FILES map both earn their keep; allowJs lets the test share the browser's render.js.
The API response spreads paginate() including `offset`; harmless and documented, but trimming would tighten the contract.
getCustomers casts node:sqlite rows with `as unknown as Customer[]` — the standard pragmatic pattern for the untyped API, localized to one line. Frameworks justified; lint + knip clean.
README steps run verbatim with no undocumented steps; / , /health, /api/customers all behave as documented.
README documents requirements, run steps, API contract, architecture diagram, module responsibilities, and trade-offs; provenance recorded in docs/tailwind-plus-sources.md.
packageManager pinned; deps minimal and justified; generated DB and styles.css git-ignored (git status clean after build); commits well-scoped and test-first.
Minor doc staleness: .gitignore comments reference the old script name `import` instead of `db:import`. Harmless comment drift.
